event.summary: Prepared a source-first, planning-last push bundle without executing Git push.

acceptance.proof: A named test inserts a second CRLF between the canonical identifier delimiter and a valid LSLC-002A body, then asserts the complete-envelope body-start offset and unchanged delegated LSLC-002A NonCanonical error.

Crate-level prose names the bounded local source-only response-envelope contract while excluding endpoint behavior/networking, and ownership_declaration owns the response-envelope contract with a regression assertion.
Public routing and validation history record the two primary-review gaps without changing or rewriting accepted LSLC-002F evidence or behavior.
No correlation, sockets, discovery runtime, endpoints, clocks/currentness, interoperability, activation, devices, dependencies, unsafe/FFI, private artifacts, or Manifold authority are added.

document.commit_policy: Add one narrow correction after published LSLC-002F; preserve its commits, receipt, push plan, and review history without amendment, rebase, reset, or force push.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Record the additive LSLC-002G acceptance correction and unchanged non-scope.
Record the primary rejection and corrected public API/status ownership surface.
Review public/private routing; no shared-skill change required.
Review additive failure evidence, ownership declaration, and closed activation; no shared-skill change required.

non_scope: Any encoder/parser behavior change unless the explicit regression exposes a defect; query-response correlation semantics, sockets, UDP, multicast, discovery runtime, endpoint ownership or selection, retries, clocks, provider/currentness evidence, or ecosystem interoperability.
Dependencies, features, unsafe, FFI, devices, runtime activation, private artifacts, copied implementation source, or external oracle execution.
Manifold admission, registry, subscription, route, lease, session, topology, revision, replay, authorization, audit, transport, command, product, or provider authority.
document.objective: Correct LSLC-002F additively by adding the explicitly claimed extra-CRLF damaged regression and synchronizing crate-level status and ownership declarations with the existing bounded local source-only response-envelope API, without changing envelope behavior or widening authority.
outputs: Named extra-CRLF test with exact complete-envelope offset and unchanged delegated LSLC-002A error.
Synchronized crate documentation, ownership declaration, and ownership regression assertion.
Public rejection history, focused checker, Standard receipt, and no-force executed feature-push receipt.
